title: The Adventures of Toto – Hard SAQs

questions.question: What qualities of Toto made him attractive to Grandfather?
questions.answer: His bright eyes, cleverness, and playful nature attracted Grandfather.

questions.question: How does the author highlight Toto’s intelligence?
questions.answer: Through his clever actions like opening things and imitating human behaviour.

questions.question: Why did Grandfather admire Toto even after the damage he caused?
questions.answer: He admired Toto’s cleverness and lively nature.

questions.question: What does the railway incident reveal about rules and humour?
questions.answer: It shows how rules can sometimes lead to funny misunderstandings.

questions.question: How does Toto’s behaviour disturb other animals?
questions.answer: He keeps them awake and troubles them with his mischief.

questions.question: What does Toto biting Nana’s ears suggest?
questions.answer: It shows his aggressive and playful nature.

questions.question: How does Toto’s bathing habit show his intelligence?
questions.answer: He carefully checks the water temperature and imitates human bathing behaviour.

questions.question: Why does Toto run to the kitchen fire after bathing?
questions.answer: To dry himself quickly.

questions.question: What lesson do we learn from the kettle incident?
questions.answer: Curiosity without caution can be dangerous.

questions.question: Why did Toto’s behaviour cause fear among family members?
questions.answer: Because he often broke things and behaved unpredictably.

questions.question: What does the lunch-time incident reveal about Toto?
questions.answer: It reveals his greedy and mischievous nature.

questions.question: Why did Toto throw dishes at the family members?
questions.answer: He reacted mischievously when people tried to stop him.

questions.question: How does the jackfruit tree scene add humour to the story?
questions.answer: Toto calmly eats the rice while the family watches helplessly.

questions.question: Why does Toto break the dish after eating?
questions.answer: He does it out of mischief and revenge against Grandmother.

questions.question: What message does the story give about keeping wild animals?
questions.answer: Wild animals may not adapt well to domestic life.

questions.question: How does Ruskin Bond use humour in the story?
questions.answer: He uses funny situations and Toto’s actions to create humour.

questions.question: What does Toto’s behaviour symbolise?
questions.answer: Uncontrolled curiosity and mischief.

questions.question: Why did the family tolerate Toto for some time?
questions.answer: Because Grandfather loved him and found him amusing.

questions.question: Why was selling Toto the final solution?
questions.answer: Because the family could not handle his destructive behaviour.

questions.question: How does the story show the difference between wild and domestic animals?
questions.answer: Toto’s behaviour proves that wild animals are difficult to control as pets.

questions.question: What type of narrative style is used in the story?
questions.answer: First-person narrative style.

questions.question: What makes the story entertaining?
questions.answer: The humorous incidents caused by Toto.

questions.question: How does the author portray Toto’s cleverness?
questions.answer: Through his quick actions and ability to imitate humans.

questions.question: Why is the story considered humorous?
questions.answer: Because it describes funny and unexpected situations created by Toto.

questions.question: What is the overall message of the story?
questions.answer: Wild animals are difficult to keep as pets despite their charm.
